summary: The test suite is comprehensive and well-structured, covering all five functional areas (CSV loading, filtering, dept aggregates, overall stats, report generation) as required by the task. TDD cycles are clearly delineated by class, fixtures are well-designed (both an in-memory SAMPLE_EMPLOYEES dict and a file-backed FIXTURE_CSV), and pytest.approx is used correctly for float assertions. The end-to-end smoke test in test_full_pipeline_with_fixture_csv ties the pipeline together. Rigor is strong but not perfect: there is no test for malformed CSV rows (e.g., a non-numeric salary value that should raise an error), no explicit test that status normalisation works for mixed-case inputs like 'Active' or 'ACTIVE' (the code claims case-insensitive behaviour but tests only supply pre-lowercased data), and no boundary test for a CSV with only a header and no data rows. Salary-zero and duplicate-name edge cases are also unexamined. Design is excellent — fixtures are reusable and DRY, tests are independent, tmp_path is used correctly for file I/O, and docstrings clearly explain intent. The coupling of hardcoded row counts (16 total, 12 active) to the fixture file is acceptable given that the fixture is part of the test suite. Overall this is a trustworthy suite that would catch the vast majority of regressions.
